text:
5000m runners at theParisOlympicsavoided a near-calamitous moment after a cameraman casually walked onto the track during a race.

The heats for the men’s 5000m took place at the Stade de France on Wednesday morning, but the two races were marred by athletes colliding and falling over.

Team GB star George Mills was left fumingafter being tripped and confronted French rival Hugo Hay after eventually crossing the finishing line in the first heat.

Follow Metro.co.uk’s live blog for updates from the Paris 2024 Olympics

But there were even more shocking scenes 10 minutes into the second heat when the leading pack ran around the first turn, only to find a clueless cameraman strolling across the track.

The cameraman seemed blissfully unaware of the runners and had his back turned to them when they ran around him.

â ï¸ Watch out! Cameraman about! ð¥#Olympics#Paris2024#BBCOlympicspic.twitter.com/oXvA2GWB3S

Thankfully no one was tripped over, but Jakob Ingebrigtsen of Norway was visibly livid, gesturing toward the cameraman in frustration.

During long distance races like the 5000m and 10,000m it is not uncommon to see other personnel on the track and at the same moment of this incident several javelin competitors could be see on the outskirts of the track waiting to cross.

Marshalls are on hand to ensure that no one interferes with the runners but on this occasion it seems a lapse in concentration nearly caused a near-collision.

Ingebrigtsen went onto win the head and advance to the final where he’ll be joined by Mills who has been given a grant to compete following his fall.

title: Olympics cameraman almost causes huge crash by strolling into middle of track | Metro News
description: 5000m runners at the Paris 2024 Olympics avoided a near-calamitous moment after a cameraman casually walked onto the track during a race.
